blueprint.name: Auto Lights
blueprint.description: Automatically turn lights on and off based on binary sensors (motion sensors, door sensors, etc.), with optional adaptive lighting that adjusts brightness and color temperature based on the sun position.
input.lights.name: Lights
input.lights.description: The lights to control: a single light, a group of lights, or even a Home Assistant area or label.
input.sensors.name: Turn-on and turn-off sensors
input.sensors.description: Sensors that drive automatic light turn-on and turn-off: works with motion and presence sensors, doors and windows, or any other binary sensor type. Leave empty to use adaptive lighting only as soon as the lights turn on.
input.off_delay.name: Delay before turn-off
input.off_delay.description: Delay before turning the lights off when the selected sensors above become inactive again (no more motion, door closed, etc.). If several sensors are selected, the delay starts only when all sensors are inactive.
input.keep_on_open_doors.name: Keep the light on with open doors
input.keep_on_open_doors.description: By default, door sensors turn the light on when they open, but do not keep it on and the lights will turn off after the configured delay. When this option is enabled, door sensors are treated like the other sensor types and the lights stay on as long as the doors remain open.
input.switch_entity.name: Turn-on with switch
input.switch_entity.description: The input helper selected here turns the lights on and off regardless of the situation. Useful with a physical switch: another automation can toggle this helper to turn the lights on and off. Its state is also automatically synchronized with the managed lights.
input.control_entities_off.name: Control entities
input.control_entities_off.description: The selected input helpers must be off for automatic turn-on and turn-off to work. This can be used to disable the automation when nobody is home, for example.
section.adaptive.settings.description: Optional light adaptation throughout the day.
input.enable_adaptive.name: Enable adaptive lighting
input.enable_adaptive.description: Automatically adjust brightness and color temperature based on the sun position throughout the day.
input.brightness_night.name: Minimum brightness
input.brightness_night.description: Minimum brightness around sunrise and sunset.
input.brightness_day.name: Maximum brightness
input.brightness_day.description: Maximum brightness around the middle of the day.
input.kelvin_night.name: Minimum temperature
input.kelvin_night.description: Warmest white temperature around sunrise and sunset.
input.kelvin_day.name: Maximum temperature
input.kelvin_day.description: Coolest white temperature around the middle of the day.
input.night_adaptive_enabled.name: Specific settings at night
input.night_adaptive_enabled.description: By default, the minimum values defined above are applied at night. Enable this option to use the values below instead.
input.auto_mode_boolean.name: Control entity
input.auto_mode_boolean.description: The selected input helpers must be off for adaptive lighting to run. When they are disabled, the lights keep their last setting and are still turned on and off automatically according to the settings above. Useful to disable adaptive lighting without disabling the other automation features.
input.restore_on_off.name: Automatic re-activation
input.restore_on_off.description: If enabled, the control entity above is automatically re-enabled when all managed lights turn off.
input.disable_on_color.name: Disable on color mode
input.disable_on_color.description: If enabled, the control entity above is automatically disabled when one of the managed lights switches to color mode. Requires that entity to be an input_boolean.
input.weather_entity.name: Weather entity
input.weather_entity.description: Optional weather entity used to slightly shift adaptive brightness and white temperature.
input.auto_on_mode.name: Automatic turn-on
input.auto_on_mode.description: Choose when automatic turn-on should be active. Night is based at minimum on the sun position provided by Home Assistant, with the optional criteria below available as additional inputs.
input.light_sensors.name: Illuminance sensors (optional)
input.light_sensors.description: Optionally, night mode can be enabled when the illuminance measured by the selected sensors goes below the threshold defined below. When several sensors are selected, the average is calculated automatically.
input.light_threshold.name: Illuminance threshold
input.light_threshold.description: Illuminance threshold used to switch between day mode and night mode. Ignored if no illuminance sensor has been selected above.
input.night_mode_entity.name: Night mode entity (optional)
input.night_mode_entity.description: The selected input helper also activates night mode, in addition to the sun position and illuminance when configured.
input.night_mode_brightness.name: Night-specific brightness
input.night_mode_brightness.description: Brightness applied at night when Specific settings at night is enabled.
input.night_mode_kelvin.name: Night-specific temperature
input.night_mode_kelvin.description: White temperature applied at night when Specific settings at night is enabled.
trigger.managed_light_switched_to_color_: A light switched to color mode
